item.title: Ethiopia opens insurance market to foreign investors under draft law
item.description: Ethiopia plans to allow foreign insurers for the first time in decades, aiming to boost competition, capital, and coverage

item.title: Digital insurance growth in Africa meets reinsurance capacity gap
item.description: Digital insurance expands across Africa and the Middle East, yet weak reinsurance capacity and rising motor losses raise pressure on sustainability

item.title: Kulipa raises $6.2 mn to scale stablecoin card issuing infrastructure
item.description: Paris-based Kulipa raised $6.2mn in seed funding to expand stablecoin-funded card issuing across regulated markets in Europe, Africa and the US

item.title: Santam Syndicate 1918 hires Nigel Tatlock to lead property buildout
item.description: Santam Syndicate 1918 appointed Nigel Tatlock as Head of Property as it expands its Lloyd’s platform and builds out global property underwriting

item.title: Italian startup Intuos raises €720 k to digitise general aviation fleets
item.description: Italian startup Intuos secures €720k to expand fleet management platform into the US after Europe growth and South Africa distribution deal

item.title: Congo unrest drives political violence insurance premiums up to 10x
item.description: Political violence insurance premiums in DR Congo surged up to tenfold after eastern unrest, boosting insurers while raising costs for mining operators

item.title: Cargo theft shifts online as insurers warn of rising digital freight fraud
item.description: IUMI and TAPA EMEA warn cargo crime is surging worldwide, with digital fraud replacing physical theft as losses reach billions across supply chains

item.title: 🇳🇵Nepal arrests Everest rescue officials over $20 mn insurance fraud
item.description: Nepal detains six rescue agency officials accused of staging helicopter evacuations near Mount Everest to extract nearly $20 mn from global insurers

item.title: In 🇿🇼 Zimbabwe, funeral insurance outpaces health cover as care costs soar
item.description: With healthcare unaffordable for most Zimbabweans, funeral insurance has become the dominant financial product, reflecting deep cultural priorities and systemic collapse

item.title: Health and Safety tops global D&O risk rankings as climate and AI concerns fade
item.description: Health and safety is the leading risk for directors and officers worldwide for a second year running, according to the Global D&O Survey

item.title: Santam launches Lloyd’s policy administration system with Alchemy and Buckhill
item.description: Santam, Alchemy Technology Services, and Buckhill delivered a new Lloyd’s PAS for Syndicate 1918 in under three months, replacing legacy systems with a cloud-based, ecosystem-led model.

item.title: Africa Specialty Risks renews Baobab Lloyd’s Consortium, lifts capacity to $26 mn
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks renews the Baobab Consortium at Lloyd’s, increasing per-risk capacity to $26 mn for Africa and Middle East exposures

item.title: La Niña returns, driving floods, snowstorms, and rising global losses
item.description: A renewed La Niña is fueling floods in Asia, early US snowstorms, and mounting economic losses, with insurers and commodity markets bracing for impact

item.title: Insurtech Neutrinos unveils venture studio and global “Reimagining Risk” challenge
item.description: Neutrinos launches a venture studio and a global risk-focused startup challenge to speed AI-native innovation for insurers and financial institutions

item.title: ASR launches digital cyber insurance cover with $5 mn limits for African businesses
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks rolls out a Lloyd’s-backed cyber policy via ASR 24-7, offering digital quote-and-bind access for companies across key African markets

item.title: Africa Specialty Risks launches Lloyd’s Syndicate 2454 to expand underwriting in South Africa
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks opens Lloyd’s Syndicate 2454 to write local South African risks, deepen specialty capacity and expand on-the-ground underwriting

item.title: FSD Africa sets up $30 mn 3iF fund to back early African insurtech startups
item.description: FSD Africa launches a $30 mn Inclusive Insurtech Fund to boost early-stage African insurtech, expand access and spur climate, health and financial inclusion

item.title: AXA partners with Old Mutual and EHS to expand international health cover in Africa
item.description: AXA teams with Old Mutual Kenya and EHS to grow East Africa’s IPMI market through local underwriting, exclusive distribution and portfolio transfers

item.title: Hurricane Melissa causes up to $4.2 bn insured losses in Jamaica
item.description: Verisk estimates Jamaica’s insured losses from Hurricane Melissa at $2.2–$4.2 bn, with near-total building destruction in landfall zones

item.title: Avatar MGA launches with Lloyd’s & FCA approval, backed by Santam and Lloyd’s syndicates
item.description: Avatar MGA, a managing general agent specialising in US direct and facultative (D&F) property insurance, has secured approval as a Lloyd’s coverholder and from the UK

item.title: Insurer Santam received approval from Lloyd’s to establish Syndicate 1918
item.description: South Africa-based Santam has received in-principle approval from Lloyd’s to establish a London-based syndicate, part of its strategy to expand international operations

item.title: EMEA insurers maintain strong capital positions and buffers
item.description: Insurers based in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) maintain strong capital positions and buffers, enabling them to absorb external shocks effectively

item.title: Bayer and Insurtech Pula plan to insure 10 million farmers in Africa
item.description: Bayer Foundation and insurtech Pula plan to insure 10 million smallholder farmers in Africa by 2030. This initiative protects farmers from climate risks

item.title: Insurtech Naked sequred $38 mn to drive insurance transformation in South Africa
item.description: Naked, a South African insurtech firm, has raised $38 mn in its Series B2 funding round. This represents the largest insurtech investment in Africa

item.title: Africa Specialty Risks launched Africa Specialty Risks Mauritius as a Lloyd’s company
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks (ASR), a prominent (re)insurance group targeting Africa and the Middle East, has launched Africa Specialty Risks Mauritius as a Lloyd’s service company

item.title: ASIC orders compliance overhaul for Sanlam’s Australian unit over licensing breaches
item.description: The Australian arm of South African financial services conglomerate Sanlam Group will need to have its compliance processes reviewed by an independent expert

item.title: Africa Specialty Risks expands Lloyd’s reinsurance market access for Africa
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks (ASR) announced that its United Kingdom-based unit, Africa Specialty Risks Ltd., now operates as a Lloyd’s service company

item.title: Climate insurtech IBISA raised €2.8 mn led by ARAF and Equator
item.description: Luxembourg-based climate insurtech IBISA Network has raised a €2.8 mn funding led by The Acumen Resilient Agriculture Fund (ARAF) and Equator

item.title: Appian integrated with Swiss Re’s automated life insurance underwriting in EMEA
item.description: Appian, in collaboration with Swiss Re, announced the expansion of the Connected Underwriting Life Workbench to select countries in Asia Pacific, Europe, the Middle East

item.title: Agri-insurtech Pula sequred $20 mn Series B led by BlueOrchard
item.description: Kenyan agricultural insurtech Pula has sequred $20 mn Series B funding round, led by BlueOrchard, to help thousands of smallholder farmers

item.title: Insurtech mTek raised $1.25 mn to boost expansion in African markets
item.description: Kenyan Insurtech mTek has sequred $1.25 mn in funding to boost its expansion efforts within the Kenyan and East African insurance markets

item.title: Chris Mackinnon becomes Lloyd’s regional director for Asia Pacific Middle East & Africa
item.description: Chris Mackinnon, with over three decades of experience in the insurance industry, is poised to become Lloyd’s Regional Director for Asia Pacific, Middle East, and Africa

item.title: Aon announced an increase crop insurance capacity across Africa
item.description: Aon announced a collaboration with the African Development Bank (AfDB) and Amini, a leading innovator in space technology and artificial intelligence

item.title: Verisk Maplecroft launched a predictive strikes & riots data model
item.description: Verisk Maplecroft, the risk intelligence business arm of Verisk, has launched a new predictive strikes, riots, and civil commotion (SRCC) data model

item.title: Insurtech Husmus sequred funding from Google and Innovate UK
item.description: Insurtech Husmus has raised $151K in funding from Google and British governmental agency Innovate UK

item.title: African Risk Capacity Group launches a parametric insurance for Africa
item.description: The African Risk Capacity (ARC) Group is launching a new insurance mechanism for African countries to cope with the devastating effects of flooding

item.title: Insurtech Kirontech tackles fraud & waste in medtech & scaling strategy
item.description: Medtech and insurtech company Kirontech, has announced the launch of its growth capital fundraise to help accelerate its goal of fixing the $1.2 bn problem

item.title: South African insurtech Naked Insurance raised $17 mn in Series B funding led by IFC
item.description: South African insurtech Naked Insurance has raised $17 million in Series B funding led by the International Finance Corporation (IFC)

item.title: Nigeria launches a risk transfer scheme of parametric flood insurance
item.description: Flood risk capacity development for Nigeria within the underwriting community will be key in developing appropriate flood risk models and products

item.title: 85 insurers in Africa pledge $14bn of cover to take up floods and tropical cyclones
item.description: A group of over 85 insurers in Africa have pledged to create a financing facility to provide $14 billion of cover to help protect 1.4 billion people

item.title: Allianz completes M&A a majority stake in insurance business Jubilee Holdings in East Africa
item.description: Allianz has announced that it has completed its transaction to acquire a majority stake in Jubilee Holdings Limited’s general insurance business in East Africa

item.title: Insurers distanced from EACOP, crude oil pipeline Uganda-Tanzania
item.description: A number of European insurers have ruled out underwriting a proposed crude oil pipeline linking Uganda’s oil fields with the Tanzanian port of Tanga

item.title: Kenya’s insurtech startup Lami hits $3.7mn in seed extension led by Harlem Capital
item.description: Kenya’s leading insurtech startups, Lami Technologies, has raised US$3.7mn in a seed extension round led by Harlem Capital – a VC that invests

item.title: Global estimated insured losses from natural catastrophes at $35 bn
item.description: Floods and storms drive global insured catastrophe losses of USD 38 billion in first half of 2022, Swiss Re Institute estimates

item.title: Non-insured losses on the Africa are close to 97%. Disaster Risk Financing
item.description: Only 3% of losses caused by drought, floods and tropical cyclones have been insured on the African continent

item.title: Insured losses from natural catastrophes estimated at $100bn
item.description: 1H losses would be around 40% of the 10-year average of $11.1 billion for US seasonal catastrophes in that quarter.

item.title: How insurers can help to reduce climate change impact in Africa
item.description: Through analysis, underwriting, investment and advice, insurance sector of Africa is helping to understand the complexities of climate change
